intitule: Lesson 49 Assessment: Means of Transport and Travel Vocabulary

enonce.texte: Read the sentences carefully and choose the correct option (A, B, C, or D) to complete each gap. This exercise tests your knowledge of transport and travel vocabulary from the lesson.

questions.texte: In Cameroon, many people use a ______ to travel on the Wouri River between Douala and the surrounding villages.

sousQuestions.texte: A) train B) bus C) canoe D) plane

questions.texte: Before starting a long ______ from Yaoundé to Kousseri, it is important to check your vehicle's condition.

sousQuestions.texte: A) travel B) trip C) journey D) tour

questions.texte: The minister of transport announced that the new ______ would connect Douala to Limbé in less than 45 minutes.

sousQuestions.texte: A) bus B) train C) canoe D) bicycle

intitule: Lesson 49: Distinguishing Travel, Trip, Journey, and Voyage

enonce.texte: Complete each sentence with the correct word: travel, trip, journey, or voyage. Pay attention to the context and duration.

questions.texte: Identify the correct word to fill in the blank.

sousQuestions.texte: The ______ from Bertoua to Yaoundé by road takes about 8 hours, depending on traffic.
My family and I are planning a ______ to the Waza National Park next month; we will stay there for three days.
Modern air ______ has made it possible to go from Douala to Paris in less than seven hours.
In the 15th century, a ______ across the Atlantic Ocean could take several months and was very dangerous.

enonce.texte: Situation: You are a student in Buea. Your school is organizing a field trip to the Limbe Botanic Garden. You are the class representative and must help choose the best means of transport. The group includes 45 students and 5 teachers. The road from Buea to Limbe is sometimes narrow with potholes, and the group must arrive by 10 AM. There is also heavy rain predicted for that morning.

questions.texte: Analyze the situation and justify your choice of the most appropriate means of transport for this trip.
questions.reponse: A large bus or a minibus (or two minibuses) is the most appropriate because: 1) It can carry 50 people. 2) Buses are safer on rainy roads than motorcycles or small cars. 3) It is faster than walking or using many small cars. 4) It protects students from the rain. A train is not available between Buea and Limbe. A canoe is not suitable for road transport.

sousQuestions.texte: Which means of transport would you recommend? (Choose one: A large bus, Several taxis, Motorcycles (bendskine), or A train)
Justify your choice by explaining at least three reasons related to the situation (number of people, road condition, weather, punctuality).
sousQuestions.reponse: Reason 1: Capacity – A large bus can carry all 50 people together, avoiding separation. Reason 2: Weather protection – Buses protect passengers from heavy rain, unlike motorcycles. Reason 3: Safety on bad roads – Buses are more stable on potholed roads than small cars or taxis. Reason 4: Punctuality – One bus can keep the group together and arrive on time more easily than coordinating multiple taxis.

sousQuestions.texte: What is one disadvantage of using a large bus in this specific context?
sousQuestions.reponse: Possible disadvantages: The bus might struggle on very narrow or steep parts of the road, or the cost of renting a large bus could be higher than using multiple smaller vehicles.

enonce.texte: Task: A friend from Garoua wants to visit you in Maroua. The distance is about 230 km. Your friend has three options: (1) Take a bus, (2) Take a motorbike taxi, or (3) Drive a personal car. Use your knowledge of transport vocabulary and real-life factors to help your friend decide.

questions.texte: Evaluate each option by completing the table in your mind, then answer the questions below.

sousQuestions.texte: Which option is the safest during the rainy season? Explain why.
sousQuestions.reponse: The bus is generally the safest because professional drivers are trained, buses are larger and more stable, and they are regularly maintained. A personal car can be safe if well-maintained, but a motorbike is very dangerous on slippery roads.

sousQuestions.texte: Which option is the most economical if your friend travels alone? Justify your answer.
sousQuestions.reponse: The bus is usually the most economical for a single traveler because the ticket price is fixed and cheaper than fuel, tolls, and potential repairs for a personal car. A motorbike taxi for 230 km would be very expensive and uncomfortable.

sousQuestions.texte: If your friend wants flexibility to stop at villages along the way (e.g., for lunch or sightseeing), which option is best? Why?
sousQuestions.reponse: The personal car is best because it offers complete flexibility to stop anytime, anywhere. A bus follows a fixed schedule and route and does not make unscheduled stops. A motorbike allows stops but is less comfortable and safe for long distances.

intitule: Lesson 49: Vocabulary in Context (Fill in the Blanks)

enonce.texte: Complete the following sentences using the words from the box: destination, fare, passenger, vehicle, departure, arrival.

questions.texte: Fill in each blank with the appropriate word.

sousQuestions.texte: The bus ______ from Douala is at 8 AM, so you must arrive early.
Each ______ must wear a seatbelt for safety.
The ______ to Kriba is 1500 CFA per person.
After a long drive, our final ______ was the hotel in Kriba.
